CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. 2026-04-09T11:24:59.8114180Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T11:23:27.070192587Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58000,\"title\":\"[SQL] Docs: README missing inline snippets, deprecated API migration guidance, and SQL-specific key concepts for 1.4.0\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57982) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nReleased `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 targeting API version 2025-01-01. The release includes:\\n- Upgraded API version from `package-2023-08` to `2025-01-01`\\n- `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` marked `[Obsolete]` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`\\n- Methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version marked obsolete (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2)\\n- Library declared AOT-compatible (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` defines snippet regions (`Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription`, `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection`) but the README.md contains no `C# Snippet:` placeholder blocks to inject them — so the snippets are silently ignored\\n- The `Examples` section of the README contains no inline code examples; it only links to an external page\\n- The `Key concepts` section is an empty boilerplate link with no SQL-specific content (no mention of SqlServer, SqlDatabase, ManagedInstance, ElasticPool, or the resource hierarchy)\\n- No deprecation/migration notice for `SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` → `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` in `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability`\\n- No mention anywhere in the README of the 2014-04-01 API retirement or guidance to migrate off the obsolete methods\\n- No mention of the AOT-compatibility support added in 1.4.0\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nReplace the current minimal `Key concepts` section with SQL-specific content covering the main resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` organizes SQL resources in a hierarchy that mirrors the Azure resource model:\\n\\n- **SqlServerResource** – Represents a logical SQL server (host for databases, firewall rules, etc.)\\n- **SqlDatabaseResource** – Represents a single database within a SQL server\\n- **ElasticPoolResource** – Represents an elastic pool grouping databases that share resources\\n- **ManagedInstanceResource** – Represents a SQL Managed Instance (PaaS with full SQL Server compatibility)\\n- **ManagedDatabaseResource** – Represents a database hosted on a SQL Managed Instance\\n\\nEach resource class follows the Azure SDK pattern:\\n- A `*Collection` class for listing and creating resources\\n- A `*Resource` class for reading, updating, and deleting an individual resource\\n- Extension methods on `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, and `ArmClient` to obtain collection objects\\n\\n\\u0026gt; ⚠️ **Deprecation notice (1.4.0):** `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` has been marked obsolete. Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; Several methods that relied on the retiring Azure SQL 2014-04-01 API version have also been marked obsolete. See the [Azure SQL REST API retirement notice]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for migration guidance.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the current `Examples` section with snippet placeholders and sample links:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Authenticate and get the default subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor complete sample walkthroughs, see:\\n- [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md)\\n- [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md)\\n```\\n\\nAdd an AOT note to the `Getting started` section (after the `Authenticate the Client` paragraph):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### AOT compatibility\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 and later supports [Native AOT]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) compilation.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippets in `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` are already defined correctly:\\n- `Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription` — creates an `ArmClient` and retrieves the default subscription\\n- `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection` — creates a resource group\\n\\nNo changes are needed to the test file. The snippet placeholders added in Step 1 will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently has all six required sections, but their content needs the improvements above:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present |\\n| Key concepts | ❌ Empty boilerplate |\\n| Examples | ❌ No inline code, no snippets |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for tone and formatting.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm README is updated with snippet content in the git diff\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API was changed by this doc update, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24187153008/agentic_workflow) · ● 594.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58000\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T11:20:21Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T11:20:21Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The release includes:\\n- Upgraded API version from `package-2023-08` to `2025-01-01`\\n- `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` marked `[Obsolete]` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`\\n- Methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version marked obsolete (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2)\\n- Library declared AOT-compatible (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` defines snippet regions (`Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription`, `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection`) but the README.md contains no `C# Snippet:` placeholder blocks to inject them — so the snippets are silently ignored\\n- The `Examples` section of the README contains no inline code examples; it only links to an external page\\n- The `Key concepts` section is an empty boilerplate link with no SQL-specific content (no mention of SqlServer, SqlDatabase, ManagedInstance, ElasticPool, or the resource hierarchy)\\n- No deprecation/migration notice for `SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` → `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` in `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability`\\n- No mention anywhere in the README of the 2014-04-01 API retirement or guidance to migrate off the obsolete methods\\n- No mention of the AOT-compatibility support added in 1.4.0\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nReplace the current minimal `Key concepts` section with SQL-specific content covering the main resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` organizes SQL resources in a hierarchy that mirrors the Azure resource model:\\n\\n- **SqlServerResource** – Represents a logical SQL server (host for databases, firewall rules, etc.)\\n- **SqlDatabaseResource** – Represents a single database within a SQL server\\n- **ElasticPoolResource** – Represents an elastic pool grouping databases that share resources\\n- **ManagedInstanceResource** – Represents a SQL Managed Instance (PaaS with full SQL Server compatibility)\\n- **ManagedDatabaseResource** – Represents a database hosted on a SQL Managed Instance\\n\\nEach resource class follows the Azure SDK pattern:\\n- A `*Collection` class for listing and creating resources\\n- A `*Resource` class for reading, updating, and deleting an individual resource\\n- Extension methods on `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, and `ArmClient` to obtain collection objects\\n\\n\\u0026gt; ⚠️ **Deprecation notice (1.4.0):** `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` has been marked obsolete. Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; Several methods that relied on the retiring Azure SQL 2014-04-01 API version have also been marked obsolete. See the [Azure SQL REST API retirement notice]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for migration guidance.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the current `Examples` section with snippet placeholders and sample links:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Authenticate and get the default subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor complete sample walkthroughs, see:\\n- [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md)\\n- [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md)\\n```\\n\\nAdd an AOT note to the `Getting started` section (after the `Authenticate the Client` paragraph):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### AOT compatibility\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 and later supports [Native AOT]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) compilation.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippets in `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` are already defined correctly:\\n- `Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription` — creates an `ArmClient` and retrieves the default subscription\\n- `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection` — creates a resource group\\n\\nNo changes are needed to the test file. 227,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDY0MjI3\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30464227?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/HarveyLink\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/subscriptions\"}],\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM6NwQiU\"},{\"id\":2138760952,\"number\":42027,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"[BUG] SqlDatabaseResource.SetTagsAsync() throws System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'id')\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen calling SetTagsAsync() from an SqlDatabaseResource object, the following error is thrown:\\r\\n\\r\\n\\u003e Microsoft.AspNetCore.Diagnostics.ExceptionHandlerMiddleware: An unhandled exception has occurred while executing the request.System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'id')at Azure.Core.Argument.AssertNotNull[T](T value, String name)at Azure.ResourceManager.ArmResource..ctor(ArmClient client, ResourceIdentifier id)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource..ctor(ArmClient client, ResourceIdentifier id)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource..ctor(ArmClient client, TagResourceData data)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResourceOperationSource.Azure.Core.IOperationSource\\u003cAzure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource\\u003e.CreateResultAsync(Response response, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.NextLinkOperationImplementation.OperationToOperationOfT`1.UpdateStateAsync(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationInternal`1.UpdateStatusAsync(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.UpdateStatusAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationPoller.WaitForCompletionAsync(Boolean async, OperationInternalBase operation, Nullable`1 delayHint, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\r\\n\\r\\nInterestingly, the tags are saved correctly. The error must occur after the save operation.\\r\\n\\r\\nImportant points:\\r\\n\\r\\n- The error only occurs when using a user managed identity (I host it in a Windows-based App Service).\\r\\n- When running locally using VisualStudioCredential(), no error occurs.\\r\\n- The error only occurs when Azure.ResourceManager v1.4 or later is used.\\r\\n- By default, Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 targets Azure.ResourceManager v1.3.2 - using these versions together results in no error.\\r\\n- Using Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 with Azure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 onwards causes the issue.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nI expect that I can use Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 with Azure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 onwards, and not receive an error when saving tags to a SqlDatabaseResource when accessed via a system managed identity.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nI receive the above error.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Create a .Net 6 MVC web application.\\r\\n2. Import the following NuGet package: Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0\\r\\n3. Import the following NuGet package: Microsoft.Identity.Web v2.16.1\\r\\n4. Add an AzureSql database (I have used DTU-based free tier).\\r\\n5. Create an App Service in Azure - Windows - .NET 6 runtime.\\r\\n6. Create a custom role which has \\\"Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/read\\\" and \\\"Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/write\\\" permissions.\\r\\n7. Create a system managed identity which applies the custom role on the database.\\r\\n8. Associate the system managed identity with the app service.\\r\\n\\r\\nAdd the following code into the home controller of the MVC project (fill in the todo parts with Azure info):\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\npublic async Task\\u003cIActionResult\\u003e Privacy()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    await SetTags();\\r\\n\\r\\n    return View();\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate async Task SetTags()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    SqlDatabaseResource db = await GetDatabase();\\r\\n\\r\\n    var tags = new Dictionary\\u003cstring, string\\u003e()\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        {\\\"tag1\\\", \\\"tag1val\\\"},\\r\\n        {\\\"tag2\\\", \\\"tag2val\\\"},\\r\\n    };\\r\\n\\r\\n    await db.SetTagsAsync(tags);\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate async Task\\u003cSqlDatabaseResource\\u003e GetDatabase()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    ArmClient client = GetClient();\\r\\n\\r\\n    string subscriptionId = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    string rgName = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    string serverName = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    string dbName = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n\\r\\n    ResourceIdentifier resourceId = SqlDatabaseResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, rgName, serverName, dbName);\\r\\n    SqlDatabaseResource sqlDbResource = client.GetSqlDatabaseResource(resourceId);\\r\\n\\r\\n    SqlDatabaseResource db = await sqlDbResource.GetAsync();\\r\\n\\r\\n    return db;\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate ArmClient GetClient()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    if (_hostEnvironment.IsDevelopment())\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        return new ArmClient(new VisualStudioCredential());\\r\\n    }\\r\\n\\r\\n    string managedIdentityClientId = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    return new ArmClient(new ManagedIdentityCredential(managedIdentityClientId));\\r\\n}\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nNow publish the project to the App Service.\\r\\n\\r\\nAt this point, running the code via Visual Studio, or via the App Service will write the tags to the DB correctly when navigating to the Privacy link (no errors).\\r\\n\\r\\nNow Add the following NuGet package:\\r\\nAzure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 (or above).\\r\\n\\r\\nPublish the application to the App Service again.\\r\\n\\r\\nThis time, an error will be observed when clicking on the Privacy link when run in the App Service.\\r\\n\\r\\n(Although it works ok when run in Visual Studio still).\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nVisual Studio for running locally.\\r\\nAzure App Service - Windows - .NET 6.0.\\r\\nAzure SQL Database - 5 DTU free DB.\\r\\nCustom Role with read and write permissions on SQL databases (details above).\\r\\nUser Managed Identity applying the custom role to the DB.\\r\\n\",\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"LaurenceFrost\",\"id\":23263122,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIzMjYzMTIy\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/23263122?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/LaurenceFrost\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/LaurenceFrost/
